methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Remedy Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Bell that has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including peer-stage and adjacent-stage benchmarks: 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M Series B), and Fairmat ($350M Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $35M total capital raised as a proxy baseline, implying a $70M valuation. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects stage and business-model heterogeneity within robotics, with several Series B companies commanding substantially higher valuations.

Triangulating these methods produces a low-to-high range of $60M to $1.2B, with a median of $572M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound at $70M, appropriate for an early-stage capital raise, while peer-median analysis pulls the range significantly higher. Key uncertainties include Remedy's unit economics relative to Series B-stage peers, path to profitability, and addressable market definition within the broader robotics sector. The wide range reflects material differences in growth trajectory and commercial traction among available comparables.
